source_pr.title: statistics: avoid using infoschema when to init stats
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #55851

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Test flaps around stats-lite initialization: `ColAndIdxExistenceMap` content/assumptions differ depending on infoschema version used during InitStatsLite.
failure_signature: TestBuiltinInEstWithoutStats: stats-lite init leads to unstable expectations about column existence map
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on assumptions about which columns are recorded in `ColAndIdxExistenceMap` immediately after `InitStatsLite`. That initialization previously depended on an infoschema argument/version, which could lead to inconsistent existence-map contents across runs. The fix makes stats-lite initialization not require passing infoschema (`InitStatsLite(ctx)`), and removes the brittle per-column `Has(...)` assertion so the test no longer depends on unstable internal bookkeeping.
fix_pattern: Avoid passing potentially stale infoschema into stats-lite initialization; use the updated `InitStatsLite(ctx)` API and remove brittle assertions that depend on internal existence-map details.

evidence: files: pkg/domain/domain.go, pkg/executor/infoschema_reader_test.go, pkg/executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go, pkg/meta/model/table.go, pkg/planner/cardinality/selectivity_test.go, pkg/planner/cardinality/trace_test.go, pkg/planner/core/integration_test.go, pkg/statistics/BUILD.bazel, pkg/statistics/handle/autoanalyze/priorityqueue/analysis_job_factory_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/bootstrap.go, pkg/statistics/handle/handle.go, pkg/statistics/handle/handletest/handle_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/storage/json.go, pkg/statistics/handle/storage/read.go, pkg/statistics/handle/storage/read_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/storage/stats_read_writer.go, pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload.go, pkg/statistics/handle/types/interfaces.go, pkg/statistics/handle/updatetest/update_test.go, pkg/statistics/integration_test.go, pkg/statistics/table.go, tests/realtikvtest/statisticstest/statistics_test.go
